messages.content:
During a chemical reaction ____________ cannot
be created or destroyed.

During a chemical reaction, mass cannot be created or destroyed. This is known as the law of conservation of mass. The total mass of the reactants before the reaction is equal to the total mass of the products after the reaction.

Fill in the Blanks

An exothermic reaction will cause a(n)
____________ in temperature
An exothermic reaction will cause a decrease in temperature.

The prefix end- means _________, while the suffix
–therm means _____________.
The prefix end- means "inside" or "within", while the suffix –therm means "heat" or "temperature".
A ______________ speeds up a chemical reaction.
A catalyst speeds up a chemical reaction.

In an exothermic reaction, energy is
______________.
In an exothermic reaction, energy is released.

What property is observed when deciding whether
a reaction is endothermic or exothermic?
____________________

The property that is observed when deciding whether a reaction is endothermic or exothermic is the change in enthalpy (∆H) of the reaction. If the reaction releases energy (heat), the reaction is exothermic with a negative ∆H value. If the reaction absorbs energy, the reaction is endothermic with a positive ∆H value.

Photosynthesis is an example of a(n)
______________ reaction.
Photosynthesis is an example of an endothermic reaction as it requires energy from sunlight to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.

What name is given to reactions that pull energy in
from their surroundings? ___________________
Endothermic reactions pull energy in from their surroundings.

Would a burning log be an example of an
endothermic or exothermic reaction?
_______________________
A burning log is an example of an exothermic reaction as it releases heat and light energy into the surrounding environment.

What name is given to reactions that pull energy in
from their surroundings? ___________________
Endothermic reactions pull energy in from their surroundings.

What is the term used to describe something that
slows or stops a chemical reaction?
___________________
The term used to describe something that slows or stops a chemical reaction is inhibitor.

Venn Diagram
Compare and contrast an endothermic and
exothermic reaction
ENDOTHERMIC       EXOTHERMIC

ENDOTHERMIC
- Absorbs energy 
- Positive change in enthalpy (∆H) value
- Feels cold to the touch 
- Examples are photosynthesis, melting ice

EXOTHERMIC
- Releases energy 
- Negative change in enthalpy (∆H) value
- Feels hot to the touch 
- Examples are burning wood, rusting iron 

Similarities:
- Both are types of chemical reactions 
- Both involve rearrangement of atoms and molecules 
- Both follow the law of conservation of energy (energy cannot be created or destroyed)
